behaviors.behavior: The poll sends the previous response's toByte, not 0
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor.test.ts 'the second poll asks from the first response toByte, not 0' PASSED; e2e 103 AC-1 wire-level PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Accumulated content equals the concatenation of every delta
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor.test.ts 'content across three polls equals the concatenation' PASSED; e2e 103 (line delivered only inside a delta) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: fromByte 0 REPLACES the buffer
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'fromByte 0 REPLACES' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: fromByte === cursor APPENDS and advances the cursor to toByte
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'APPENDS and advances the cursor' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Any other fromByte is rejected; pane preserved, cursor rewound to 0
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'REJECTED without corrupting the pane' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A duplicate delta is applied exactly once
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'applying the SAME delta twice' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: The cursor is bytes, not string length (multi-byte UTF-8)
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'the cursor is BYTES, not string length' PASSED; verified to FAIL under the reviewer-suggested prev.length guard

behaviors.behavior: An empty delta preserves content by value (keeps the downstream useMemo skip)
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'empty delta at the cursor is a no-op' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: modelName is carried forward when the newest delta has none
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'a delta WITHOUT a model' PASSED; cursor.test.ts 'EARLIER delta' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: modelName is overridden by the newest delta (last occurrence wins)
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'a delta WITH a model overrides it' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A REPLACE with no model CLEARS modelName (no cross-transcript leak)
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'a REPLACE with no model CLEARS it' PASSED; verified to FAIL without the fix

behaviors.behavior: A rejected chunk leaves modelName untouched
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.accumulate.test.ts 'a REJECTED chunk leaves the model untouched' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: rotated resets buffer + cursor + fingerprint
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor-reset.test.ts 'rotated clears the buffer AND the cursor' PASSED; e2e 103 AC-2 on real Chromium PASSED

behaviors.behavior: missing resets buffer + cursor + modelName
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor-reset.test.ts 'missing clears the buffer, the model and the cursor' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A taskId switch resets the cursor AND the fingerprint
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor-reset.test.ts 'no inherited fingerprint' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A taskId switch blanks the PUBLISHED state immediately
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor-reset.test.ts 'blanks the pane IMMEDIATELY' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A late response from the previous task never lands in the new buffer
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.cursor-reset.test.ts 'in-flight response from the PREVIOUS task' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Whole-file resync every RESYNC_EVERY_POLLS polls, bounding divergence a shrink-only rotation check cannot detect
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.resilience.test.ts 'resyncs to fromByte 0 every 60th poll' PASSED; verified to FAIL with dueForResync forced false

behaviors.behavior: After a rejection the recovery poll carries fromByte 0 AND a null fingerprint
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.resilience.test.ts 'after a REJECTED chunk' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A failed poll leaves the cursor and fingerprint intact
behaviors.evidence: useTaskTranscript.resilience.test.ts 'a failed poll leaves the cursor' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: The chunk is byte-identical with and without a pre-resolved location
behaviors.evidence: session-watcher.single-walk.test.ts 'byte-identical chunk with and without' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A passed location does not re-classify read-ENOENT as fatal (CLAUDE.md rule 6)

behaviors.behavior: Discovery is unchanged without a location (missing, and an explicit null)

behaviors.behavior: The pane's residual full-content re-parse cost after the change
behaviors.reason: Correctness is covered by the existing TaskDetailPage and parser suites; the NUMBER (5.6 ms median / 11.3 ms p90, measured against the shipped parseSessionJsonl) is deliberately not asserted, because a wall-clock threshold in CI is a flake generator. Scoped out in 'What this run does NOT fix'.

behaviors.behavior: Cold-page-cache behaviour on the 131 MB transcript
behaviors.reason: The OS page cache cannot be evicted deterministically from a test. The warm figure is the conservative one: a cold read strictly worsens the fromByte:0 column and leaves the cursor column flat.

surface_verification.justification: The client half changes what the transcript pane REQUESTS and how it assembles the reply, and the rendered text is identical either way - so only a real browser against a real server can show the change happened. Isolated single-process stack: temp USERPROFILE, built client served via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR, Hono on 127.0.0.1:3849, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local on both halves. NEW client/e2e/flows/103-transcript-cursor-single-walk.spec.ts: 2/2 passed - AC-1 reads the fromByte off the WIRE via page.on('request') and proves later polls stop asking for the whole file while the pane still shows a line that only ever arrived inside a delta; AC-2 truncates the JSONL under the browser and proves the client rewinds to 0 and REPLACES. Both were verified to FAIL against a client rebuilt with fromByte pinned to 0. Pre-existing transcript specs re-run for regression, all passed: 32-transcript-live, 34-browser-refresh, 37b-bubble-lifecycle (x2), 38-jsonl-missing-transition. 7/7, exit 0.
